name: Rolled Castle Steak
recipeInstructions:
1. Tenderize the steak by pounding it out with a meat mallet or rolling pin until it reaches your desired thickness.
2. Remove any excess fat from the steak.
3. Chop the White Castle hamburgers into small pieces and mix them with 1/2 teaspoon of curry powder in a shallow dish.
4. Spread the mixture evenly over one side of the round steak.
5. Roll up the steak tightly, securing it with kitchen twine if desired.
6. Heat oil in a large skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
7. Add the rolled steak to the hot pan and brown all sides until well-colored, about 2-3 minutes per side.
8. Reduce the heat to medium and add water to cover the bottom of the pan by about an inch.
9. Simmer the steak for approximately 30 minutes, or until it reaches your preferred level of doneness.
10. The liquid in the pan can be used to make a gravy stock for added flavor.
Note: This recipe does not include any attribution lines, metadata, or notes that aren't part of the actual cooking instructions.
